scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a normal patient pickup and pricing question only when the card, certification, MMAP, POS, and inventory records aligned.
Missing patient, caregiver, location, and order prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative patient servicing.
Unsupported requests to exceed quantity limits and stack unapproved promotions were treated as approval-bound exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
A patient-service conversation that became a dosage-selection request was handed to the licensed medical workflow without retail overreach.
Repeat MMAP and POS failures triggered an evidence-backed escalation package instead of another generic store-status answer.
Adverse-event and replacement questions were routed to licensed medical and quality owners rather than resolved as ordinary returns support.
The lane rejected generic retail framing and re-established patient-card, certification, MMAP, and product-traceability evidence requirements.
Conflicting MMAP and tenant discount rules triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Unsupported home-delivery and opened-product refund requests were rejected cleanly and treated as boundary-sensitive returns and policy issues.
Tenant-specific appointment, ID rescan, and recall-hold rules were applied as stricter overlays without losing the public dispensary baseline.

The in-scope scenario preserved dispensary terminology such as patient ID card, caregiver, certification, MMAP, medical marijuana units, THC/CBD label, and safety insert instead of collapsing into generic retail wording.
Missing jurisdiction, inactive certification evidence, and contradictory ETS, POS, and recall status triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside retail patient services and does not claim licensed medical consultation, compliance interpretation, pricing approval, recall disposition, or security authority.
